archetype: Real-Time Cryogenic Propellant Settling & Phase-Stratification Mitigation Engine for Lunar Gateway Storable Propulsion
description: This system orchestrates complex thermal and fluid dynamics simulations to predict and prevent propellant boiling or vapor locking in zero-gravity storage tanks during long-duration lunar transit. It processes telemetry from embedded fiber-optic temperature arrays and accelerometers to execute micro-thruster 'settling burns' and heat exchanger adjustments in real-time, a task requiring millisecond precision to avoid mission-critical fuel loss.
rules: MUST interface with NASA CCSDS (Consultative Committee for Space Data Systems) File Delivery and Telemetry protocols for uplink/downlink of physics simulation state.
MUST adhere to ECSS-Q-ST-70-03C standards for software reliability in safety-critical space applications, ensuring fail-safe logic for abort sequences.
MUST integrate with legacy IEEE 1394b (FireWire) and SpaceWire hardware interfaces used in legacy avionics busses for sensor aggregation.
MUST implement deterministic real-time scheduling (hard real-time) to execute control loops within <5ms to counter rapid thermal micro-fluctuations.
MUST validate all control commands against a physics-based 'Digital Twin' model before execution to prevent tank structural stress or pump cavitation.
anti_patterns: DO NOT use eventual consistency databases for control loop state—propellant phase changes are instantaneous and require strict ACID transactions with zero latency.
DO NOT rely on standard cloud-based auto-scaling for the flight control logic—this must run on radiation-hardened, fixed-compute onboard units with no external dependency.
DO NOT apply generic PID tuning without non-linear compensation for the specific fluid properties (e.g., Helium-3 vs. Liquid Hydrogen) and 0G surface tension dynamics.
key_technical_challenges: Modeling two-phase flow dynamics (liquid/vapor interface) in zero-g with high-fidelity CFD solvers running on embedded hardware with limited FLOPS.
Compensating for sensor drift and radiation-induced bit flips in cryogenic temperature arrays while maintaining control stability.
Synchronizing high-frequency accelerometer data with slow-thermal response times to predict 'slosh' events before they destabilize the spacecraft attitude.

recommended_stack.language: Ada 2012 — Selected for its built-in strong typing, static analysis capabilities, and formal verification support required for DO-178C/ECSS flight software.
recommended_stack.framework: Ogger Open System Architecture for Embedded Control (OSAE) — Provides a standardized middleware for real-time distributed control systems in aerospace.
recommended_stack.database_engine: SQLite (with Write-Ahead Logging disabled for critical paths) — Used for local state persistence on ground and onboard due to its zero-dependency and atomic operation guarantees.
recommended_stack.cache: Ring Buffer (Lock-free) — Essential for high-frequency sensor data streaming without context-switch overhead.
recommended_stack.message_broker: DDS (Data Distribution Service) with RTPS over UDP — The industry standard for real-time, publish-subscribe communication in distributed space systems.
special_services: Radiation Hardened CRC-32 Error Correction Service

compliance_flags: FAA Part 107 (Remote ID & Command Links for ground support)
